name: Native DB
description: Native DB is a drop-in, fast, embedded database for multi-platform Rust apps (server, desktop, mobile). It maintains coherence between Rust types and stored data with minimal boilerplate, using the native_model crate for transparent serialization/deserialization with any serde-compatible format. Native DB supports multiple indexes (primary, secondary, unique, non-unique, optional), ACID transactions via redb, automatic model migration, real-time watch subscriptions with filters, and hot snapshots. Designed for Rust developers who want type-safe persistence without defining schemas separately from their data types.
core_strengths: Zero-schema: Rust structs define the schema, no DDL or ORM mapping needed
Type-safe queries via generics prevent key-type-to-model mismatches
Automatic model migration via versioned types and From trait conversion
Multiple indexes: primary, secondary, unique, non-unique, and optional
ACID transactions via redb with read-write and read-only modes
Real-time watch subscriptions with filters for insert, update, delete
Hot snapshots for backup without stopping the database
best_use_cases: Rust desktop apps (Tauri, egui, iced) needing typed local persistence
iOS and Android Rust apps needing embedded database cross-platform
CLI tools storing structured config and state with automatic migration
Embedded systems and IoT devices needing type-coherent local storage
Single-user apps where Rust types define the entire data model
remarks: Native DB uses a single-writer, multiple-reader concurrency model backed by redb. It does not support SQL or a query language — all data access is through typed Rust API calls. Schema evolution relies on the native_model crate's versioning and the From trait for migrations. The database is a single file managed by redb. The API is still evolving and may have breaking changes between minor versions. Not suitable for multi-process access; only one writer at a time.
